title: Repository, Python Runtime, SDKs, Dependencies, and Reproduction · Guided Program Studio
summary: A public-interest product team incrementally builds reproducible Python agent repository baseline from original fixed provider, interaction, tool, approval, evaluation, and failure fixtures while retaining prior Python, HTTP, prompt, loop, security, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of reproducible Python agent repository baseline while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Inspect revision, status, existing behavior, tests, configuration, fixtures, secrets, licenses, threat model, and recovery anchor before editing.
Record Python implementation and version, platform, event-loop behavior, SSL backend, free-threaded status, encoding environment, and supported matrix.
Pin direct and resolved dependencies, verify package sources and hashes, review licenses and advisories, and prove isolated installation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Repository Baseline
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop agent engineering team handles case 9845 during a controlled support-operations rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile repository, Python, SDK, dependency, license, advisory, and clean-environment evidence; last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure; then inject cancellation, retry, repeated-call, checkpoint, or replay failure and recover without duplicate effects. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect revision, status, existing behavior, tests, configuration, fixtures, secrets, licenses, threat model, and recovery anchor before editing.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Clean Reproduction
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-clean-reproduction-ykypmp" that uses case 4033 to reconcile repository, Python, SDK, dependency, license, advisory, and clean-environment evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-clean-reproduction-ykypmp
# Competency: agentpy-clean-reproduction.
# Case 4033: a multilingual case-routing evaluation.
# Operating constraint: telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable.
# Changed case: inject cancellation, retry, repeated-call, checkpoint, or replay failure and recover without duplicate effects.
def evidence_agentpy_clean_reproduction_ykypmp(declared_dependencies: int = 7, resolved_dependencies: int = 7, clean_installs: int = 3, supported_python_versions: int = 2, unresolved_advisories: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ykypmp = "ykypmp-pmpyky-4033"
    assert evidence_variant_ykypmp
    evidence_axes_ykypmp = (
        "authorityykypmp",
        "interaction4033",
        "toolpmpyky",
        "stateykypmp4033",
        "evaluation4033ykypmp",
        "recoverypmpyky4033",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ykypmp)) == 6
    if declared_dependencies < 1 or resolved_dependencies < 1:
        return False, resolved_dependencies, "dependency-shape"
    if resolved_dependencies != declared_dependencies:
        return False, resolved_dependencies, "dependency-drift"
    if clean_installs < 2 or supported_python_versions < 1:
        return False, clean_installs, "reproduction-gap"
    if unresolved_advisories > 0:
        return False, unresolved_advisories, "advisory-gap"
    assert resolved_dependencies == declared_dependencies and clean_installs >= 2
    return True, clean_installs, "reproducible-agent-baseline"
